verified: project-original procedural production and graybox road-visual profiles selected through one generator contract
shared materials and meshes for pavement, shoulders, terrain, markings, reflectors, concrete barriers, guardrails, posts, delineators, guide signs, route shields, exit-only panels, and service panels
fixture-derived bridge deck and overpass opening assembled from shared decks, girders, piers, abutments, and deck barriers without duplicate collision
stable semantic metadata across nine observed representative-interchange chunks, eleven structure nodes, and every route-context waypoint
variable-lane, entrance, exit, highway-transfer, branch-prewarm, local-origin, and gore topology regressions
renderer-backed 1280x720 route-context, bridge, daylight, and night review capture
completion_pending: Q-022 minimum target PC and quantitative renderer budgets
production terrain, roadside furniture, weathering, decals, and regional asset rights records
exact sign typography and shield-shape quality pass plus quantitative daylight and night high-speed renderer budgets
excluded_claims: MUTCD or CDOT traffic-control engineering compliance
production-complete art, regional authenticity, licensed highway typography, or final asset rights approval
ratified draw-call, GPU frame-time, texture-residency, LOD, or minimum-PC budgets

acceptance_comparisons.criterion: production and graybox visuals preserve the same procedural and semantic contract
acceptance_comparisons.observed: both profiles resolved the same nine chunks, eight route-context roots, one bridge deck and overpass opening, eleven structure nodes, 18 materials, nine meshes, 626 reflectors, 199 barrier segments, 199 guardrail segments, six shields, two service panels, and two lighting stages

acceptance_comparisons.criterion: the technical kit survives representative lane and interchange topology
acceptance_comparisons.observed: the aggregate gate passed two-to-four-lane transitions, gore geometry, entrance and highway-transfer movements, all four interchange plans, fourteen connectors, branch prewarm and eviction, and local-origin rebases

acceptance_comparisons.criterion: route-context signage is renderer-reviewable without losing stable automation identity
acceptance_comparisons.observed: the six-waypoint 60 FPS capture validated each declared label visibility envelope, returned to the exact bridge route position after a rebase, and preserved daylight and night structure frames in a durable contact sheet

output_artifacts.retention: local review artifact; the tracked contact sheet is durable

failure_logs.failure: The first combined profile incorrectly required gore geometry to appear on the transfer-plan route-context traversal itself.
failure_logs.recovery: The road-visual contract now validates the shared kit and signage on the transfer plan, while the aggregate gate proves gore geometry on the dedicated variable-lane topology fixture.

failure_logs.failure: The first structure review framed the bridge after its road chunk had been unloaded and the world had rebased, making the valid structure look disconnected from the road.
failure_logs.recovery: The review scenario now returns to the structure's exact upper-edge route distance, waits for streaming to settle, and then frames the rebased bridge in daylight and night.

recovery_result: All autonomous technical, semantic, topology, bridge/overpass, daylight/night renderer, and regression gates for the current procedural baseline pass; production art, quantitative performance, rights, and human readability approval remain explicitly open.

human_gate.name: Highway art readability, visual quality, and final rights approval
